---
title: |
    | 理解“共同富裕”的认知维度
    | 一个政治心理学探索
subtitle: "比较政治与治理系列讲座·北京大学"
date: "2026-05-13"

author: 
    - 胡悦
institute:
    - 清华大学社会科学学院

bibliography: p_inequality_compare.bib

format: 
    revealjs:
        css: https://www.drhuyue.site/slides_gh/css/style_basic.css
        theme: ../../css/goldenBlack.scss
        slide-number: true
        filters: [appExclusion.lua] # not count appendices into page number
        incremental: false
        preview-links: true # open an iframe for a link
        link-external-newwindow: true
        self-contained: false
        chalkboard: false # allowing chalk board B, notes canvas C
        # callout-icon: false
        show-slide-number: all # `speaker` only print in pdf, `all` shows all the time
        title-slide-attributes:
            data-background-image: https://gitlab.com/sammo3182/backup/raw/85b3c1ad4b459d7a9f901f124b936428eda5fcaf/logo_THPS.png?inline=true
            data-background-size: 250px   
            data-background-position: top 10% right 5%
        default-image-extension: png
revealjs-plugins:
    - spotlight
lightbox: 
    match: auto
    effect: fade
spotlight:
    size: 50
    presentingCursor: default
    toggleSpotlightOnMouseDown: false
    spotlightOnKeyPressAndHold: 73 # keycode for "i"

editor:
    render-on-save: true
    chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
#| include: false
#| label: setup
library(pacman)

p_load(
  "icons"
  #   "tidyverse",
  #   "drhutools"
)

# Functions preload
# set.seed(313)

# theme_set(
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 18)
# )

# theme_update(
#   plot.title = element_text(size = 18),
#   axis.title = element_text(size = 22),
#   axis.text = element_text(size = 18)
# )
```


## The Requirement of the national development

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="40%"}

![2021年1月11日，中共中央总书记、国家主席、中央军委主席习近平在省部级主要领导干部学习贯彻党的十九届五中全会精神专题研讨班在中央党校（国家行政学院）开班式上发表重要讲话。Source:共产党员网](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_xijinping.png){fig-align="center" height=400}



:::

::: {.column width="60%"}

> [实现共同富裕不仅是经济问题，而且是**关系党的执政基础的重大政治问题**……要自觉主动解决地区差距、城乡差距、收入差距等问题，推动社会全面进步和人的全面发展，促进社会公平正义，让发展成果更多更公平惠及全体人民，不断增强人民群众获得感、幸福感、安全感，**让人民群众真真切切感受到共同富裕不仅仅是一个口号，而是看得见、摸得着、真实可感的事实**。]{.red} [@XiJinPing2021d]


::: {.fragment style="text-align:center"}

**&darr;**

Contribute to the Common Wealth from a *political-psychological* perspective

:::


:::

::::


## The potential cognitive risk

- The world is more unequal than at any point since World War II
- Yet mass demand for redistribution has *not* risen to match [(Why?)]{.fragment .red}

::: {.fragment}

> **Economic inequality**: The uneven distribution of wealth (e.g., [income]{.red}) among individuals or groups within a society.

:::

::: {.incremental .fragment}

- **How** does living in an unequal society shape citizens' sociopolitical minds?
- **How &rarr; When**: through what mechanisms do macro inequality reach the micro mind?
- **When &rarr; What if**: what happens when exposure to inequality becomes easier?

:::

::: {.notes}

Roadmap for the talk: three linked questions, two countries (US + China), one claim --- inequality changes minds toward indifference and even conservatism, not toward demand for redistribution.

:::

## What we have known about inequality

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

*On the social fabric* [@Cozzolino2011; @PayneEtAl2017]

- [&uarr;]{.blue} risk-taking, homicide, obesity, teenage births
- [&darr;]{.red} interpersonal trust, child well-being, education, life expectancy

*On democracy* [@Bartels2008; @Gilens2014; @SchlozmanEtAl2013; @Solt2008]

- [&darr;]{.red} engagement, representation, responsiveness

:::

::: {.column .fragment width="50%"}

*And on stability*: The **social-volcano**

Inequality [&uarr;]{.blue} &rarr; unrest [&uarr;]{.blue} &rarr; regime stability [&darr;]{.red} [@He2003a]

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_socialVolcano.webp){fig-align="center" height=380}

:::

::::


## Unsolved puzzles

What we've known: *Inequality is bad for minds*

- How does macro inequality reach the micro mind?
- Why don't citizens in unequal societies demand more redistribution? [@BelguiseEtAl2025; @HoyMager2021; @Whyte2010; @Whyte2016]

::: {.r-hstack}

![Don't know/care [@Bottero2019] Source: Generated by ChatGPT](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_dontCare.png){.fragment fig-align="center" height=350}

![Believe things will get better [@HirschmanRothschild1973;@AndersonSinger2008] Source: 《我的要求不算高》](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_tunnelEffect.jpg){.fragment fig-align="center" height=350}

:::


::: {.notes}

- Tunnel effect: 当你看到旁边车道开始移动时，会感到希望而非愤怒，期待自己也即将前进

:::

## My answer

::::{.overlay-container}

::: {.overlay-text}

The standard answers strain when:

- the economy *develops slower* --- the wall is no longer hidden, and
- people *come to know* --- exposure to inequality is no longer optional.

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_wallCollapse.webp){.fragment fig-align="center" height=450}

:::

::: {.overlay-text-over .fragment}

Economic inequality changes minds &rarr; mass [indifference, even conservatism]{.red}, in redistribution preferences.

:::

::::

::: {.notes}

This is the thesis of the talk. The rest of the slides build it in three steps:

1. *How*: macro inequality reshapes class consciousness and meritocratic belief (US).
2. *When/why*: it travels through perceptions of government performance (China).
3. *What if*: making inequality unavoidable, via social comparison, still does not produce uniform demand for redistribution --- effects depend on where you sit in the institution.

:::

## Two cases, one puzzle

::: {.r-stack}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_gini_US.png){fig-align="center" height=600}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_gini_ChinaUS.png){.fragment fig-align="center" height=600}

:::

::: {.notes}

面对快速变化的世界和中国，如果墨守成规、思想僵化，没有理论创新的勇气，不能科学回答中国之问、世界之问、人民之问、时代之问，不仅党和国家事业无法继续前进，马克思主义也会失去生命力、说服力。——习近平总书记在省部级主要领导干部学习贯彻党的十九届六中全会精神专题研讨班开班式上（2022年1月）

- 0.4 Gini: the richest 40% control about 60~70% of the fortune
- 0.6 Gini: the richest 20~30% control about 70~80%
- US for the *How* question (long Pew time series, established theory); China for *When/why* and *What if* (rising inequality + institutional variation among state, SOE, and private sectors).

:::

# How

How does living in an unequal society shape citizens' minds about their social roles?

## Economic inequality &rarr; social role

::::{.overlay-container}

::: {.overlay-image}

::: {.r-hstack}

![Source: Reddit·r/AutisticUnion](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_classConsciousness.webp){.fragment fig-align="center" height=500}

![Source: *Zootopia* (2016)](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/mod_zootopia.gif){.fragment fig-align="center" height=500}

:::

:::

::: {.overlay-text-over .fragment}

- **Conflict theory**: under high inequality, the poor have *higher* class consciousness and believe in meritocracy [less]{.red}.
- **Relative power theory**: under high inequality, the poor do *not* gain class consciousness, and may believe in meritocracy [more]{.blue}.

:::

::::

:::{.notes}

- Class: bourgeoisie /bou-zhua-zi/, proletariat
- Meritocracy is the key for the American Dream and the hope of the poor
    - Self-reinforce: relative power theory
    - Self-negating: conflict theory

- photo sources
    - https://www.reddit.com/r/AutisticUnion/comments/1dy10wo/what_was_youre_class_consciousness_moment/
    - https://sipofstarrshine.com/2016/03/15/zootopia-2016/
:::


## Examining on the U.S. case

*Data*: Pew survey, national data of the U.S. (1999--2012)

$$
\begin{align}
\color{red}{Reject}\thinspace Meritocracy_{ij} =\boldsymbol{X\gamma} &+ \gamma_{10}Income_{ij} + \gamma_{01}Inequality_{j}\\
        &+ \gamma_{11}Inequality_{j} \times Income_{ij} + u_{ij}.
\end{align}
$$


:::{.r-hstack}

![@SoltEtAl2016](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_class1.png){.fragment fig-align="center" height=300}

![@SoltEtAl2017](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/mod_SH_plot2.png){.fragment fig-align="center" height=300}

:::

::: {.callout-important .fragment}

## Take-away 

Higher inequality &rarr; *less* class-conscious and *more* attached to meritocracy

:::

::: {.notes}

Self-negating vs. self-reproducing

Relative power theory gets support:

1. Economic inequality [reduces]{.red} people's class consciousness
1. Economic inequality [reduces]{.red}  people's belief in meritocracy

Have-nots [@NewmanEtAl2015]: Respondents were asked the following question: "Some people think of American society as divided into two groups, the 'haves' and the 'have-nots,' while others think it's incorrect to think of America that way. Do you, yourself, think of America as divided into haves and have-nots, or don't you think of America that way?

:::

# When (why)

Through what mechanisms do macro inequality reach the micro mind?

## From "what is" to "what should be"

**Perceiving** ahead of **judging**：If inequality reshapes minds, it likely does so through *evaluation*, not bare awareness [@KrieckhausEtAl2014]

![Source: China Value Survey (2021)](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_factPerceive.png){.fragment fig-align="center" height="450"}

::: {.notes}

- 贵港：广西壮族自治区
- 扬州/南京：江苏
- 抚州：江西省

:::

## People can feel inequality in *various* ways [@JinEtAl2024]

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.r-vstack}

![Impartial governance](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_collusion.jpg){fig-align="center" height=250}

![Government Non-responsiveness](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_nonresponse.jpg){.fragment fig-align="center" height=250}

:::

:::{.notes}

Impartial governance: 

- The damage of economic disparities on political equality in democracies has been well documented in the literature [@SchlozmanEtAl2013]. 
- Formal and informal institutions exist in non-democracies to convert economic resources into political influence and favorable treatment. 
- Wealthy Chinese private entrepreneurs form extensive connections with local government officials to gain privileges [@ChenDickson2010]. 

Government Non-responsiveness: 

- Substantial evidence indicates that economic inequality reduces social capital and generates distrust and social conflicts. 
- Class conflict theory suggests that in highly unequal societies, people's perceptions of economic positions on the social ladder are accentuated [@KrausEtAl2013]. 
- Low levels of social capital and accentuated social conflicts make governance difficult as @BaldwinHuber2010 showed that income differences make an agreement over which public goods to provide more difficult, therefore have a negative relationship with public goods provision [@EppJennings2020]. 

:::

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.r-vstack}

![Judicial Injustice](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_judicialInjustice.jpg){.fragment fig-align="center" height=250}

![Anti-Corruption Inadequacy](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_anticorruption.jpg){.fragment fig-align="center" height=250}

:::


:::{.notes}

Judicial Injustice:

1. greater inequality implies that the well-off have more resources to control the legal apparatus while the poor are more disadvantaged.
1. the advantaged are motivated to attempt such control because of intensified social conflicts. 

Anti-Corruption Inadequacy:

When inequality leads to widespread corruption, citizens doubt governments' intentions and abilities to combat corruption and perceive governments as incompetent to address the problems of the system. 

人言包相是铁面，却原来官官相护有牵连

- photo links:
    - https://ncnewsline.com/2020/04/27/scales-of-injustice/

:::

:::

::::



## Examining on the Chinese Case [@JinEtAl2024]


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
Data:

- *Chinese Household Finance Survey* 2013
- *China Urban Governance Survey* 2015

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

Method: *Mediation analysis*

+ Multi-mediator estimation
+ central and local gov

:::

::::

:::{.notes}

Imai 2014, mediation analysis for causal mechanism and non binary treatment

:::


![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/mediation.png){.fragment fig-align="center" height=350}


::: {.callout-important}

## Take-away

The macro reaches the micro via the *closest* institution.

:::


# When &rarr; What if

What happens when the wall comes down --- when people are *forced* to compare?

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_wallCollapse.webp){fig-align="center" height=380}


## Comparison &rarr; Perception

> There exists, in the human organism, a drive to evaluate his opinions and abilities. The person will do this by [comparing himself with others]{.red}. [@Festinger1954, p. 117]

:::{.fragment}

- Upward comparison (vs. the rich) &rarr; discontent [@Crosby1976; @Gurr2015]
- Downward comparison (vs. the poor) &rarr; self-esteem [@Wills1981; @HopkinsBilimoria2008; @GibbonsGerrard1989]

:::{.callout-note .fragment}

## Comparison Effects

$H_{1.1}$: Upward comparison &rarr; redistribution support [&uarr;]{.red}

$H_{1.2}$: Downward comparison &rarr; redistribution support [&darr;]{.green}

:::

:::

## Institutional conditions


::: {.r-hstack}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_riceBowl.webp){fig-align="center" height=450}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_soe.png){fig-align="center" height=450}

:::

::: {.fragment}

$H_{3.1}$: Governmental and SOE employees &rarr; [lower]{.blue} redistribution support    
$H_{3.2}$: The masses &rarr; [higher]{.red} redistribution support

::: 

::: {.notes}

@WangYunGuang2024

- 体制内：4835.9万，4.8 million，0.3 percent
- 国企：3054万，3 million，0.2 percent
- Difference: 体制内被cooperated，国企insecurity

:::

## Experimental Design [Inspired by @CondonWichowsky2020]

::::{.overlay-container}

::: {.overlay-text}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="70%"}

> [In Chinese] Given the social ladder on the left, please imagine the individuals in Chinese society who are the [(worst/best) off, (level 1/10)]{.red}---those who have the (least/most) wealth, (lowest/highest) education level, and (least respected/most respected) jobs. Specifically, [consider how you differ from these individuals]{.red} in terms of your own income, educational background, and job status.

:::

::: {.column width="30%"}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/macArthorLadder.jpg){fig-align="center" height=600}

:::

::::

:::


::: {.overlay-text-over .fragment}

Policy preferences:

Which of the following policies can help alleviate social and economic inequality and promote common prosperity?
Please rank the policies below **in order of importance** (You don't have to select all):

_______ Increasing the minimum living allowance (Dibao)    
_______ Raising retirement pensions    
_______ Raising unemployment benefits    
_______ Increasing the amount of education loans

:::

::::



:::{.notes}

Participants in the control group will be asked to imagine conversing with individuals who have similar income, education levels, and social status as themselves, and to reflect on what topics might arise in such a conversation.

:::

## Policy preferences w.o. social comparison

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_unitPolicy.png){fig-align="center" height=600}

::: {.notes}

- Dibao is the highest policy, just like air conditioner
- PGO is somewhat stands align with the ordinary people

:::

## Experimenting Social Comparison in China

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

- Data: China Value Survey (2021)
    - Nationally (quota) representative
    - N: 3,603

::: {.fragment}

- Post-experimental adjustment
    - Sex, age, education, urban/rural, marital status, party member
    - GDP, GDP growth rate, population, city-level Gini
    - Provincial fixed effect

:::

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_surveyIncome.png){fig-align="center" height=450}

:::

::::


## Results

::: {.r-stack}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_p2.png){fig-align="center" height=600}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_compare_unit.png){.fragment fig-align="center" height=600}

:::

::: {.notes}

No effects but in the same direction

:::

## Explanation 1: Self vs. others

!["How do you feel about the partner in the imagined conversation"](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_self.png){fig-align="center" height=600}

::: {.notes}

没有比处于自己位置的人更苦的了。

:::

## Explanation 2: Insider vs. outsider

::: {.r-stack}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_result_expectation.jpg){fig-align="center" height=600}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_result_policy.png){.fragment fig-align="center" height=600}

:::

::: {.notes}

1. PGO中低收入部分觉得更公平、代际流动可能更高，但高收入部分则不是
2. SOE才对政策有诉求还是只针对本类人群的诉求
3. Mass并没有多想

:::

## Wrap up

::::{.overlay-container}

::: {.overlay-text}

- "How": Inequality provides an environment that people's (esp. the poor's) sociopolitical cognition can be easier to be manipuated
- "When": Inequality reach individual minds through close feelings and perceptions
- "What if": Cognitive factors nested in institutional structures has kept the "social volcano" dormant, but people are [neither unaware nor indifferent]{.red}.

::: {.callout-tip .fragment}

## Open questions

1. Alternative explanations for the "what if" findings?
1. What can the government do based on the findings?
1. What's the role of techniques in this process?
......

:::

:::


::: {.overlay-text-over .large .fragment}

> [共同富裕是一个长远目标，需要一个过程，不可能一蹴而就，对其长期性、艰巨性、复杂性要有充分估计，办好这件事，等不得，也急不得。]{.red}
> [@XiJinPing2021c]

:::

::::

::: {.notes}

China does not have a property tax or a capital gains tax.

:::



# Thank you {background="#43464B"}

:::{style="text-align: right; margin-top: 1em"}  

[`r feather_icons("github")`&nbsp; sammo3182](https://github.com/sammo3182)

[`r feather_icons("mail")`&nbsp; yuehu@tsinghua.edu.cn](mailto:yuehu@tsinghua.edu.cn) 

[`r feather_icons("globe")`&nbsp; https://www.drhuyue.site](https://www.drhuyue.site)

![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6463211/232207708-b0e64eee-7fb3-45a4-9779-ec52397f786c.png){height=250}

:::

---

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/poster_tang.png){fig-align="center" height=600}

## Reference

::: {#refs}
:::

# Appendix {.appendix visibility="uncounted"}

## Estimating Inequality &rarr; meritocracy/class awareness

*Target*

> Which statement do you agree:

- Most people who want to get ahead can make it if they’re willing to work hard
- Hard work and determination are no guarantee of success for most people.

*Model*

$$
\begin{align}
\color{red}{Reject}\thinspace Meritocracy_{ij} =\boldsymbol{X\gamma} &+ \gamma_{10}Income_{ij} + \gamma_{01}Inequality_{j}\\
        &+ \gamma_{11}Inequality_{j} \times Income_{ij} + u_{ij}.
\end{align}
$$


## Marginal Effects on Class Consciousness and Meritocracy

::: {.r-hstack}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_class2.png){fig-align="center" height=600}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/spec_interaction2.jpg){.fragment fig-align="center" height=600}

:::

## Examining on the China Case


:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
Data:

*Chinese Household Finance Survey* (CHFS) 2013

+ 28,141 households from 29 provinces 
+ Representative income data for Gini coefficient

*China Urban Governance Survey* (CUGS) 2015

+ 3,513 from 50 prefectures 
+ Later than CHFS
+ Political perceptions
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

Measurement:

- Governance &rarr; Perceptions of government equality in treating citizens
- Responsiveness &larr; External political efficacy
- Justice &larr; Trust in the courts’ and procuratorates’ fairness
- Anti-corruption &larr; Satisfaction of anti-corruption


Method: *Causal mediation analysis*

+ Multi-mediator estimation
+ central and local governments

:::

::::

:::{.notes}

Imai 2014, mediation analysis for causal mechanism and non binary treatment

:::


## Measurement of Social Comparison (CVS 2021, N = 3,603)

:::{.r-stack}

- Outcome
    - Inequality perception: income distribution fairness (1--4)
    - Preference in redistribution policies
        - Minimum subsistence allowance (*dibao*) &larr; benefit the [poor]{.green}
        - Unemployment insurance &larr; benefit the [poor]{.green}
        - Education loans &larr; benefit the [middle]{.blue}/[poor]{.green}
        - Pension &larr; benefit the [middle]{.blue}/[rich]{.red}
- Income: Low-middle-high ([official categorization](https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/202102/t20210228_1814177.html))
- Co-optation: governmental or SOE employed
- Control: 
    - Gender, age, education, urban/rural, party member
    - GDP, GDP growth rate, population, city-level Gini
:::


:::{.notes}

the per capita disposable income of low-income group reached 7,869 yuan, the lower-middle-income group 16,443 yuan, the middle-income group 26,249 yuan, the upper-middle-income group 41,172 yuan and the high-income group 80,294 yuan. 

:::

## Descriptive statistics of the social comparison project

::: {.r-stack}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_descStats1.png){fig-align="center" height=600}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_descStats2.png){.fragment fig-align="center" height=600}

:::

## Further readings

::: {.r-hstack}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_economicOther.jpg){fig-align="center" height=600}

![](https://drhuyue.site:10002/sammo3182/figure/inequality_Jinshuai.jpg){fig-align="center" height=600}

:::

::: {.notes}

- Shuai：the volcano exists and fulled with both objective inequality and the government's response to it 
- Whyte: 公众对不平等的容忍主要来自功绩制信念、发展叙事与可上升预期，而非对分配结果本身的满意。
    - 社会位置对态度的影响稳定且城市更批判、农村更宽容的格局基本延续，但这并不否认特定议题上的活跃不满

:::
